description: I was on my own, walking back across the ice, looking at the array of gulls before me, sun to back. I'd picked out two GW x HERG and then saw another similar bird, but this bird had wingtips that were concolorous with the remainder of the wing. As I lifted my camera and aimed, it took off, so I only have flight shots. The bird was a stout gull, shorter winged and thicker billed than a HERG. It was nearly entirely brown, uniform in darkness, when perched, lighter than HERGs nearby. The bill was mostly black, with a tad of pink at very base. For flight, see photos which accurately depict the bird.
